sentences.sentence: In late 1946 or early 1947, three Bedouin teenagers were tending their goats and sheep near the ancient settlement of Qumran, located on the northwest shore of the Dead Sea in what is now known as the West Bank.
sentences.translation: 在1946年末或1947年初，三名贝都因少年正在古代聚落库姆兰附近照看山羊和绵羊；库姆兰位于死海西北岸，也就是如今所称的约旦河西岸地区。

structure.text: were tending their goats and sheep
near the ancient settlement of Qumran
located on the northwest shore of the Dead Sea
in what is now known as the West Bank

difficult_points: 句首时间短语限定故事发生的大致时间。
located... 是过去分词短语，修饰 Qumran。
what is now known as the West Bank 是 what 引导的名词性结构，作介词 in 的宾语。
were tending 是过去进行时，表示当时正在照看牲畜。

expressions.text: were tending their goats and sheep

sentences.sentence: One of these young shepherds tossed a rock into an opening on the side of a cliff and was surprised to hear a shattering sound.
sentences.translation: 其中一名年轻牧羊人把一块石头扔进悬崖侧面的一个洞口，听到破裂声时感到很惊讶。

structure.text: tossed a rock into an opening on the side of a cliff
was surprised to hear a shattering sound

difficult_points: One of 后接复数 these young shepherds，但谓语用单数概念。
tossed 和 was surprised 是并列谓语，共用同一主语。
to hear... 表示惊讶的原因。
shattering sound 指陶罐等物体破碎的声音。

sentences.sentence: He and his companions later entered the cave and stumbled across a collection of large clay jars, seven of which contained scrolls with writing on them.
sentences.translation: 后来，他和同伴进入洞穴，偶然发现了一批大型陶罐，其中七个装有写着文字的卷轴。

structure.text: and stumbled across a collection of large clay jars
seven of which contained scrolls with writing on them

difficult_points: entered 和 stumbled across 是顺序动作，说明进入洞穴后有了发现。
stumbled across 表示偶然发现，而不是故意寻找。
seven of which 中 which 指 large clay jars。
them 指 scrolls，表示卷轴上有文字。

sentences.sentence: The teenagers took the seven scrolls to a nearby town where they were sold for a small sum to a local antiquities dealer.
sentences.translation: 这些少年把七卷卷轴带到附近的一座城镇，在那里它们以一小笔钱卖给了一名当地古董商。

structure.text: took the seven scrolls to a nearby town
where they were sold for a small sum to a local antiquities dealer
they were sold for a small sum

difficult_points: where 引导定语从句，修饰 a nearby town。
they 指 the seven scrolls。
were sold 是被动语态，说明卷轴被出售。
for a small sum 表示售价很低。

sentences.sentence: Word of the find spread, and Bedouins and archaeologists eventually unearthed tens of thousands of additional scroll fragments from 10 nearby caves; together they make up between 800 and 900 manuscripts.
sentences.translation: 这一发现的消息传开后，贝都因人和考古学家最终从附近10个洞穴中发掘出数以万计的额外卷轴碎片；这些碎片合起来构成了800到900份手稿。

structure.text: Bedouins and archaeologists eventually unearthed tens of thousands of additional scroll fragments from 10 nearby caves
tens of thousands of additional scroll fragments
together they make up between 800 and 900 manuscripts

difficult_points: Word of the find spread 中 Word 表示“消息”。
unearthed 是考古语境中的“发掘出”。
分号后 together they... 总结这些碎片构成的手稿数量。
they 指 additional scroll fragments。

sentences.sentence: It soon became clear that this was one of the greatest archaeological discoveries ever made.
sentences.translation: 很快，人们就清楚地意识到，这是有史以来最伟大的考古发现之一。

structure.text: that this was one of the greatest archaeological discoveries ever made
one of the greatest archaeological discoveries ever made

difficult_points: It 是形式主语，真正内容是 that 引导的从句。
this 指死海古卷的发现。
one of 后接复数 discoveries。
ever made 是过去分词短语，表示“有史以来做出的”。

expressions.text: one of the greatest archaeological discoveries
expressions.meaning_zh: 最伟大的考古发现之一

sentences.sentence: The origin of the Dead Sea Scrolls, which were written around 2,000 years ago between 150 BCE and 70 CE, is still the subject of scholarly debate even today.
sentences.translation: 死海古卷的起源至今仍是学术争论的主题；这些古卷大约写于2000年前，即公元前150年至公元70年之间。

structure.text: The origin of the Dead Sea Scrolls
which were written around 2,000 years ago between 150 BCE and 70 CE

difficult_points: 主语核心是 The origin，不是 Dead Sea Scrolls。
which 引导非限制性定语从句，补充说明古卷年代。
is still the subject of... 表示某问题仍在被讨论。
even today 强调争论延续到现在。

sentences.sentence: According to the prevailing theory, they are the work of a population that inhabited the area until Roman troops destroyed the settlement around 70 CE.
sentences.translation: 根据主流理论，它们是一个人群的作品；这个人群一直居住在该地区，直到公元70年前后罗马军队摧毁了该聚落。

structure.text: are the work of a population
until Roman troops destroyed the settlement around 70 CE

difficult_points: they 指 The Dead Sea Scrolls。
the work of a population 表示古卷是某一群体创作或抄写的成果。
that 引导定语从句，修饰 population。
until 从句说明这个群体居住在当地的时间终点。

sentences.sentence: The area was known as Judea at that time, and the people are thought to have belonged to a group called the Essenes, a devout Jewish sect.
sentences.translation: 该地区当时被称为犹太地区，而这些人被认为属于一个名为艾赛尼派的群体，这是一个虔诚的犹太教派。

structure.text: The area was known as Judea at that time
the people are thought to have belonged to a group called the Essenes

difficult_points: was known as 表示“被称为”。
are thought to have belonged to 是被动表达，表示学界推测这些人曾属于某群体。
called the Essenes 是过去分词短语，修饰 group。
a devout Jewish sect 是 the Essenes 的同位语。

expressions.text: are thought to have belonged to

sentences.sentence: The majority of the texts on the Dead Sea Scrolls are in Hebrew, with some fragments written in an ancient version of its alphabet thought to have fallen out of use in the fifth century BCE.
sentences.translation: 死海古卷上的大多数文本是希伯来语，其中一些碎片使用的是希伯来字母的一种古老形式，据认为这种字母形式在公元前5世纪已经不再使用。

structure.text: The majority of the texts on the Dead Sea Scrolls
with some fragments written in an ancient version of its alphabet
written in an ancient version of its alphabet
thought to have fallen out of use in the fifth century BCE

difficult_points: The majority of... 是主语，表示“大多数……”。
with 结构补充说明部分碎片的文字形式。
its alphabet 中 its 指 Hebrew。
thought to have fallen out of use... 是过去分词短语，说明这种字母形式被认为已停止使用。

sentences.sentence: But there are other languages as well.
sentences.translation: 但也有其他语言。
difficult_points: But 与上一句“大多数是希伯来语”形成转折。
there are 是存在句结构，真正的信息中心是 other languages。
as well 表示“也”，说明古卷不只使用希伯来语。

sentences.sentence: Some scrolls are in Aramaic, the language spoken by many inhabitants of the region from the sixth century BCE to the siege of Jerusalem in 70 CE.
sentences.translation: 一些卷轴使用亚拉姆语，这种语言从公元前6世纪到公元70年耶路撒冷被围期间，由该地区许多居民使用。

structure.text: the language spoken by many inhabitants of the region
spoken by many inhabitants of the region
from the sixth century BCE to the siege of Jerusalem in 70 CE

difficult_points: the language... 是 Aramaic 的同位语，解释这种语言。
spoken by... 是过去分词短语，修饰 language。
from... to... 表示该语言在这一时期被使用。
the siege of Jerusalem in 70 CE 是时间范围的终点。

sentences.sentence: In addition, several texts feature translations of the Hebrew Bible into Greek.
sentences.translation: 此外，几篇文本包含希伯来圣经的希腊语译本。

structure.text: translations of the Hebrew Bible into Greek

difficult_points: In addition 用来添加另一种语言相关信息。
feature 在这里是动词，表示“包含、以……为特色”。
translations of... into Greek 表示从希伯来圣经译成希腊语的文本。

sentences.sentence: The Dead Sea Scrolls include fragments from every book of the Old Testament of the Bible except for the Book of Esther.
sentences.translation: 死海古卷包含《圣经·旧约》中除《以斯帖记》之外每一卷书的片段。

structure.text: fragments from every book of the Old Testament of the Bible
except for the Book of Esther

difficult_points: include 后说明古卷包含哪些圣经文本片段。
from every book... except for... 表示除一个例外外几乎覆盖全部。
except for the Book of Esther 是排除结构。

sentences.sentence: The only entire book of the Hebrew Bible preserved among the manuscripts from Qumran is Isaiah; this copy, dated to the first century BCE, is considered the earliest biblical manuscript still in existence.
sentences.translation: 在库姆兰出土的手稿中，唯一完整保存的希伯来圣经书卷是《以赛亚书》；这份抄本可追溯到公元前1世纪，被认为是现存最早的圣经手稿。

structure.text: The only entire book of the Hebrew Bible preserved among the manuscripts from Qumran
preserved among the manuscripts from Qumran
this copy, dated to the first century BCE, is considered the earliest biblical manuscript still in existence
dated to the first century BCE

difficult_points: 第一分句主语很长，核心是 The only entire book。
preserved... 是过去分词短语，修饰 book。
分号后 this copy 指 Isaiah 这份抄本。
is considered... 是被动语态，表示学界评价。

sentences.sentence: Along with biblical texts, the scrolls include documents about sectarian regulations and religious writings that do not appear in the Old Testament.
sentences.translation: 除了圣经文本之外，这些卷轴还包括关于教派规章的文件，以及未出现在《旧约》中的宗教著作。

structure.text: documents about sectarian regulations and religious writings
that do not appear in the Old Testament

difficult_points: Along with 表示“除了……之外还”。
documents 和 religious writings 是 include 的并列宾语。
that do not appear... 修饰 religious writings。
sectarian regulations 指某一宗教团体内部的规章。

sentences.sentence: The writing on the Dead Sea Scrolls is mostly in black or occasionally red ink, and the scrolls themselves are nearly all made of either parchment (animal skin) or an early form of paper called 'papyrus'.
sentences.translation: 死海古卷上的文字大多使用黑色墨水，偶尔使用红色墨水；卷轴本身几乎全都由羊皮纸（动物皮）或一种叫作“纸草”的早期纸张制成。

structure.text: The writing on the Dead Sea Scrolls
is mostly in black or occasionally red ink
the scrolls themselves are nearly all made of either parchment (animal skin) or an early form of paper called 'papyrus'

difficult_points: writing 指卷轴上的文字，不是“写作行为”。
the scrolls themselves 强调卷轴的材质本身。
are made of either... or... 表示两类材料二选一。
called 'papyrus' 是过去分词短语，修饰 an early form of paper。

sentences.sentence: The only exception is the scroll numbered 3Q15, which was created out of a combination of copper and tin.
sentences.translation: 唯一的例外是编号为3Q15的卷轴，它由铜和锡的组合制成。

structure.text: which was created out of a combination of copper and tin

difficult_points: The only exception 与上一句“几乎全由羊皮纸或纸草制成”形成对比。
numbered 3Q15 是过去分词短语，修饰 scroll。
which 指 the scroll numbered 3Q15。
created out of 表示用某种材料制成。

expressions.text: a combination of copper and tin

sentences.sentence: Known as the Copper Scroll, this curious document features letters chiselled onto metal – perhaps, as some have theorized, to better withstand the passage of time.
sentences.translation: 这份奇特的文件被称为“铜卷轴”，其特点是文字被凿刻在金属上——也许正如一些人推测的那样，是为了更好地经受时间流逝。

structure.text: to better withstand the passage of time

difficult_points: Known as... 是过去分词短语，说明文献名称。
features 在这里是动词，表示“具有……特点”。
chiselled onto metal 修饰 letters，说明文字的制作方式。
破折号后的 perhaps...to... 表示对金属刻字目的的推测。

sentences.sentence: One of the most intriguing manuscripts from Qumran, this is a sort of ancient treasure map that lists dozens of gold and silver caches.
sentences.translation: 作为库姆兰出土的最引人入胜的手稿之一，它像是一种古代藏宝图，列出了几十处金银贮藏点。

structure.text: One of the most intriguing manuscripts from Qumran
is a sort of ancient treasure map
that lists dozens of gold and silver caches
dozens of gold and silver caches

difficult_points: 句首 One of... 是同位说明，介绍 this 的身份。
this 指 Copper Scroll。
a sort of 表示“某种、类似一种”。
that 引导定语从句，修饰 ancient treasure map。

expressions.text: one of the most intriguing manuscripts
expressions.meaning_zh: 最引人入胜的手稿之一

sentences.sentence: Using an unconventional vocabulary and odd spelling, it describes 64 underground hiding places that supposedly contain riches buried for safekeeping.
sentences.translation: 它使用非常规词汇和奇特拼写，描述了64处地下藏匿地点，据称那里藏有为安全保管而埋下的财富。

structure.text: Using an unconventional vocabulary and odd spelling
that supposedly contain riches buried for safekeeping

difficult_points: Using... 是现在分词短语，说明文本描述时使用的语言特征。
it 指 Copper Scroll。
that 引导定语从句，修饰 hiding places。
buried for safekeeping 修饰 riches，说明财富被埋藏的目的。

sentences.sentence: None of these hoards have been recovered, possibly because the Romans pillaged Judea during the first century CE.
sentences.translation: 这些宝藏没有一处被找回，可能是因为罗马人在公元1世纪洗劫了犹太地区。

structure.text: because the Romans pillaged Judea during the first century CE

difficult_points: None of these hoards 表示这些宝藏一个也没有。
have been recovered 是现在完成时的被动语态。
possibly 表明原因只是推测。
because 引导原因状语从句，说明一种可能解释。

sentences.sentence: According to various hypotheses, the treasure belonged to local people, or was rescued from the Second Temple before its destruction or never existed to begin with.
sentences.translation: 根据各种假说，这些宝藏可能属于当地人，或者是在第二圣殿被毁前从那里抢救出来的，又或者一开始就从未存在过。

structure.text: or was rescued from the Second Temple before its destruction
or never existed to begin with

difficult_points: According to various hypotheses 表明后面是多种假说，不是定论。
belonged、was rescued、existed 是三个并列可能性。
its destruction 中 its 指 the Second Temple。
to begin with 表示“从一开始”。

sentences.sentence: Some of the Dead Sea Scrolls have been on interesting journeys.
sentences.translation: 一些死海古卷经历过有趣的流转历程。

structure.text: Some of the Dead Sea Scrolls

difficult_points: Some of... 表示只讨论部分古卷。
have been on journeys 是比喻说法，指古卷经历了多次转手和迁移。
interesting journeys 为本段古卷买卖和回归耶路撒冷的故事做铺垫。

sentences.sentence: In 1948, a Syrian Orthodox archbishop known as Mar Samuel acquired four of the original seven scrolls from a Jerusalem shoemaker and part-time antiquity dealer, paying less than $100 for them.
sentences.translation: 1948年，一位名为 Mar Samuel 的叙利亚东正教大主教从耶路撒冷的一名鞋匠兼兼职古董商那里购得最初七卷中的四卷，花费不到100美元。

structure.text: a Syrian Orthodox archbishop known as Mar Samuel
acquired four of the original seven scrolls
from a Jerusalem shoemaker and part-time antiquity dealer

difficult_points: known as Mar Samuel 是过去分词短语，修饰 archbishop。
acquired 表示获得、购得。
four of the original seven scrolls 表示最初七卷中的四卷。
paying... 是现在分词状语，补充说明购买花费。

sentences.sentence: He then travelled to the United States and unsuccessfully offered them to a number of universities, including Yale.
sentences.translation: 随后他前往美国，并把这些卷轴提供给多所大学，包括耶鲁大学，但没有成功。

structure.text: then travelled to the United States
and unsuccessfully offered them to a number of universities

difficult_points: He 指 Mar Samuel。
then 表示时间顺序，承接购买古卷之后的行动。
them 指四卷原始古卷。
including Yale 补充说明 universities 中包括耶鲁。

sentences.sentence: Finally, in 1954, he placed an advertisement in the business newspaper The Wall Street Journal – under the category 'Miscellaneous Items for Sale' – that read: 'Biblical Manuscripts dating back to at least 200 B.C. are for sale.
sentences.translation: 最后，在1954年，他在商业报纸《华尔街日报》上刊登了一则广告，放在“杂项出售”类别下，广告写道：“可追溯到至少公元前200年的圣经手稿正在出售。

structure.text: he placed an advertisement in the business newspaper The Wall Street Journal
under the category 'Miscellaneous Items for Sale'
'Biblical Manuscripts dating back to at least 200 B.C. are for sale
dating back to at least 200 B.C.

difficult_points: Finally 标志这段流转故事进入最后出售阶段。
破折号之间的 under the category... 是插入说明广告分类。
that read 修饰 advertisement，表示广告内容写着什么。
dating back to... 是现在分词短语，修饰 Biblical Manuscripts。

sentences.sentence: This would be an ideal gift to an educational or religious institution by an individual or group.'
sentences.translation: 这将是个人或团体送给教育机构或宗教机构的理想礼物。”

structure.text: an ideal gift to an educational or religious institution

difficult_points: This 指广告中出售的 Biblical Manuscripts。
would be 是委婉推销语气，表示“会是”。
to an educational or religious institution 表示礼物的接受对象。
句末引号表示广告引文结束。

sentences.sentence: Fortunately, Israeli archaeologist and statesman Yigael Yadin negotiated their purchase and brought the scrolls back to Jerusalem, where they remain to this day.
sentences.translation: 幸运的是，以色列考古学家兼政治家 Yigael Yadin 谈成了购买事宜，并把卷轴带回耶路撒冷；它们至今仍留在那里。

structure.text: Israeli archaeologist and statesman Yigael Yadin
and brought the scrolls back to Jerusalem
where they remain to this day

difficult_points: Fortunately 表示作者对古卷回归耶路撒冷的评价。
archaeologist and statesman 是 Yigael Yadin 的双重身份。
their purchase 中 their 指这些卷轴。
where 引导非限制性定语从句，修饰 Jerusalem。

sentences.sentence: In 2017, researchers from the University of Haifa restored and deciphered one of the last untranslated scrolls.
sentences.translation: 2017年，海法大学的研究人员修复并破译了最后几卷尚未翻译的卷轴之一。

structure.text: researchers from the University of Haifa
one of the last untranslated scrolls

difficult_points: restored and deciphered 是并列动作，表示先修复再解读。
one of 后接复数 scrolls，表示“最后未译卷轴之一”。
untranslated 表示此前尚未被翻译或解读。
from the University of Haifa 修饰 researchers。

expressions.text: one of the last untranslated scrolls
expressions.meaning_zh: 最后几卷未译卷轴之一

sentences.sentence: The university's Eshbal Ratson and Jonathan Ben-Dov spent one year reassembling the 60 fragments that make up the scroll.
sentences.translation: 该大学的 Eshbal Ratson 和 Jonathan Ben-Dov 花了一年时间重新拼合构成这卷古卷的60个碎片。

structure.text: The university's Eshbal Ratson and Jonathan Ben-Dov
spent one year reassembling the 60 fragments

difficult_points: The university's 指海法大学的两位研究人员。
spent one year reassembling 是 spend time doing 结构。
that 引导定语从句，修饰 fragments。
make up 在这里表示“组成、构成”。

sentences.sentence: Deciphered from a band of coded text on parchment, the find provides insight into the community of people who wrote it and the 364-day calendar they would have used.
sentences.translation: 这一发现是从羊皮纸上一条编码文字带中破译出来的，它让人们深入了解写下它的群体，以及他们可能使用过的364天历法。

structure.text: Deciphered from a band of coded text on parchment
the community of people who wrote it
and the 364-day calendar they would have used

difficult_points: Deciphered... 是过去分词短语，说明 the find 的来源和解读方式。
provides insight into 表示“使人深入了解”。
who wrote it 修饰 people，it 指该卷轴或文字。
they would have used 表示推测这些人当时可能使用过的历法。

sentences.sentence: The scroll names celebrations that indicate shifts in seasons and details two yearly religious events known from another Dead Sea Scroll.
sentences.translation: 这卷古卷列出了标志季节变化的庆祝活动，并详细说明了从另一卷死海古卷中已知的两个年度宗教事件。

structure.text: and details two yearly religious events
known from another Dead Sea Scroll

difficult_points: names 和 details 都是动词，构成并列谓语。
that indicate... 修饰 celebrations。
shifts in seasons 指季节变化。
known from... 是过去分词短语，修饰 two yearly religious events。

sentences.sentence: Only one more known scroll remains untranslated.
sentences.translation: 已知卷轴中只剩下另外一卷尚未翻译。
difficult_points: Only 放在句首修饰 one more known scroll，强调数量只剩一卷。
remains 是系动词，后接形容词 untranslated。
untranslated 表示尚未被翻译或解读。
